QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_70

question.text: When the aggregate open interest in a stock derivative across exchanges exceeds 95% of MWPL, what action is taken?

answer.text: No fresh positions are permitted from the next trading day; normal trading resumes only after aggregate OI falls to 80% or below MWPL
Trading is immediately halted and all existing positions are compulsorily squared off
Only index hedging positions are allowed; all speculative positions are banned
The exchange increases MWPL by 20% to accommodate the higher OI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At 95% of MWPL, a ban on fresh positions is imposed from the next trading day. Only square-off/closing transactions are permitted. The ban lifts when aggregate OI falls to ≤ 80% of MWPL. Note: alert is triggered at 60% of MWPL. **The Trap:** Existing positions are NOT forcibly closed. Only new positions are banned. And the threshold is 95% (not 100%). **Cross-Exam Context:** MWPL breach protocol is a regulatory fact tested in VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 60% = alert. 95% = ban on new positions. 80% = ban lifted. Existing positions continue.
